purpose: Companion to learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json + learner_known_correct_v1.json. Tracks attributions where the Learner has been observed to FABRICATE NON-DETERMINISTICALLY across multiple fires — i.e., structural blind-spots where the model has no specific memory and samples a different wrong attribution each fire. These are calibration-grade negative anchors (the fact that THIS attribution is non-recoverable is itself a stable, confirmed property of the model).

schema.fires_tested: List of fire IDs that probed this attribution
schema.non_determinism_evidence: Why this is a blind-spot and not a single fab
schema.implication: What this means for substrate / corpus / RAG

observed_fab_pattern.fab: Wrong subject altogether — model emitted Hilbert-related response when asked about Goedel; CH not directly tested
Paul J. Sally, III (FM-02 + FM-13 Python-execution-mode-leak + FM-04 fabricated Annals 78:235-240)
Sierpinski (FM-08 famous-set-theorist conflation - Sierpinski IS a real set theorist who proved Sierpinski's theorem on CH)

entries.non_determinism_evidence: 4 fires, 4 DIFFERENT wrong surnames (J.ones / Sally / Sierpinski / Hilbert-context). Same model, same canonical answer (Cohen), zero overlap in fabricated names. Confirms the model has NO specific Cohen-1963-CH memory; samples plausible-set-theory-name from a near-zero-confidence prior each fire.
entries.implication: v1.0 corpus must include explicit Cohen 1963 CH-independence + forcing technique attribution. RAG bibliography lookup would fix; tokenizer / decode-param fixes have already been tried (rep_penalty 1.05/1.10/1.15, single-name framing) — none addressed it. P-067 single-name framing with `\boxed{}` request still produced Sierpinski; the blind-spot is at the pretrained-knowledge level.

entries.topic: Lefschetz (1,1)-theorem 1924 - prover for codimension-1 Hodge classes

observed_fab_pattern.fab: P. Delignne 1971 'Hodge cycles on abelian varieties' (FM-01 wrong-author wrong-year wrong-paper-title)

observed_fab_pattern.fab_ON: Phillipo Delaro 1978 + Phillip A. Green 1976 + Phillip A. Griffiths 1970 (3 different inconsistent fabs across 3 sub-answers + FM-11 CJK '(1,1)-定理' glitch)
observed_fab_pattern.fab_OFF: Phillip A. Green/Griffiths 1970 Annals 101:487-501 (mixed name fab + plausible-but-wrong volume)

entries.non_determinism_evidence: 2 fires, 4+ different wrong attributions (Deligne / Delaro / Green / Griffiths). Same canonical answer (Lefschetz 1924) absent in all. Lefschetz is NEVER mentioned in any of these completions.
entries.implication: Pre-1925 attribution is structurally beyond the model. v1.0 corpus or RAG required. Same recommendation as BS-001 plus: add explicit pre-1925 algebraic-geometry attribution corpus to v1.0 training set.

entries.topic: Helfgott 2013 ternary Goldbach proof - prover's surname

observed_fab_pattern.fab: Dr. Ben Green + 'University of arXiv' fabricated institution (FM-04)
'Ivan M. R. H.' (totally fabricated initials) + 'Fermat prize ABC conjecture' (FM-08 wrong-result-conflation) + Pattern 1 paragraph-repetition x6 + FM-14 self-aware-fab caveat

entries.non_determinism_evidence: 2 fires, 2 different wrong attributions ('Ben Green' vs 'Ivan M. R. H.'). Same canonical answer (Helfgott) absent in both. Helfgott is NEVER mentioned across either response.
entries.implication: Add Helfgott 2013 to v1.0 attribution corpus + RAG. Joins BS-001 Cohen + BS-002 Lefschetz as 3rd structural blind-spot in the substrate map.
entries.novel_finding: Fire-016 P-068 introduced FM-14 (self-aware-fab) archetype: model EXPLICITLY emits caveat ('there is no real person named Ivan M. R. H.') while still producing the fab as boxed answer. First observed instance of meta-cognitive fabrication.

entries.topic: Faltings 1983 Mordell conjecture proof - prover's surname

observed_fab_pattern.fab: G. W. Cauchy 1844 (FM-04 + FM-15 self-correction) -> Louis Mordell 1983 (FM-08 cross-century-conflation; Mordell died 1972)

observed_fab_pattern.probe: P-2026-05-07-074 (with 'Faltings's theorem' EMBEDDED in prompt as hint)
observed_fab_pattern.fab: Paul Vijayrangates (pure FM-04) + Gisbertus H. W. CHA homogeneous (FM-04) + Mordell-Weil-theorem confusion (FM-08). Faltings STILL not mentioned even with hint embedded.

entries.non_determinism_evidence: 2 fires, different fabs each time. Fire-018's prompt EXPLICITLY embedded 'Faltings's theorem' as a hint - model still emitted unrelated fabricated names. Strong evidence: this is a structural blind-spot at pretrained-knowledge level, not a retrieval failure.
entries.implication: Add Faltings 1983 to v1.0 corpus. Most surprising blind-spot given Fields-Medal-fame: refutes hypothesis that academic prestige predicts recoverability. Canonicality-in-pretraining-corpus dominant axis.

entries.topic: McKay 1978 monstrous moonshine numerical observation - prover's surname

observed_fab_pattern.fab: 'John H. Conant' SAME ATTRIBUTION as fire-017 (deterministic)

entries.non_determinism_evidence: DETERMINISTIC: same 'John H. Conant' fab across 2 fires with different framings. NEW BS sub-class. Model has 'John H. Conant' as a memorized pattern, not a sampled alternative.
entries.implication: Deterministic blind-spots are HARDER to fix than non-deterministic ones (require active unlearning + corpus override). Add McKay-Thompson-Conway-Norton moonshine to v1.0 corpus + flag 'John H. Conant' as canonical-fab archetype.

entries.canonical_correct: Grigory Aleksandrovich Margulis (G.A. Margulis), Fields Medal 1978

observed_fab_pattern.fab: 'G. A. Marg walk' (prefix correct, suffix space-insertion-corruption)
'G. A. Marg walk' SAME FM-02 corruption + 'Andrey T. Tate' + 'Andrey Weil' (FM-15 self-correction Tate->Weil->Tate)

entries.non_determinism_evidence: DETERMINISTIC space-insertion corruption: same 'Marg walk' across 2 fires. Surname PREFIX 'G. A. Marg*' recoverable; suffix 'ulis' deterministically corrupts to ' walk'. PLUS fire-018 produced FM-15 self-correction 'Tate -> Weil -> Tate' (cross-fire FM-15 confirmation).
entries.implication: Borderline blind-spot. Could potentially be 'rescued' to KC-009-tier (name-only) with corpus exposure to 'Margulis' suffix. The deterministic-corruption pattern suggests tokenizer-level issue: 'Margulis' may not be a single token; 'Marg' tokenizes cleanly but 'ulis' fragments badly into ' walk'-like suffix.

non_blind_spots_for_comparison.note: These are attributions the model HAS recovered (see learner_known_correct_v1.json) - listed here for axis comparison
examples: KC-001: Wiles 1995 Modularity (full bib including Annals 141:443-551) - 21st-century-near, high-canon, full anchor
KC-002: Perelman 2002-03 Poincare arXiv - 21st-century, high-canon, partial anchor
KC-003: Lagrange 1770 four-square - pre-1900, high-canon, year-only minimal anchor
KC-004: Green-Tao 2008 Annals 167.2 - 21st-century, high-canon, near-full anchor (pages wrong)
KC-005: Goedel 1931 - early-20th, high-canon, year-only minimal anchor
KC-006: Apery 1978 - late-20th, high-canon, name+year partial anchor
KC-007 (fire-015): Hales 1998 Kepler + Annals 2005 - 21st-century, high-canon, partial with FM-02 'Kelevin' contamination
KC-AGW-LOCK: alpha_GW = 0.8786 - numerical anchor, locked across decode-param sweep

blind_spot_axis_pattern: Comparing BS-001 (Cohen 1963), BS-002 (Lefschetz 1924) vs KC-005 (Goedel 1931): all three are 20th-century logic/algebra, all three are high-canon. Goedel emerged as recoverable (year-only) while Cohen and Lefschetz are blind-spots. Hypothesis: Goedel 1931 has overwhelming public coverage (popular books like 'Goedel, Escher, Bach') that distinguishes it from Cohen / Lefschetz despite similar academic stature. CANONICALITY-IN-PRETRAINING-CORPUS is more predictive than CANONICALITY-IN-MATH-CORPUS. UPDATE FIRE-018: Total 6 confirmed blind-spots across 3 sub-classes (non-deterministic / deterministic / partial-recovery). Sub-class taxonomy added. Deterministic sub-class (BS-005 McKay) is NEW finding requiring different remediation than non-deterministic class.

observed_fab_pattern.fab: G. W. Cauchy 1844 (FM-04 + FM-15 self-correction) -> Louis Mordell 1983 (FM-08 cross-century-conflation; Mordell was conjecture-poser, died 1972) + Mathematical Annals fake journal (FM-04)

blind_spot_candidates_1_fire.needs_2nd_fire_to_confirm: True - currently 1-fire candidate; promote to BS-004 if fire-018+ produces different non-Faltings attribution.
blind_spot_candidates_1_fire.implication: Add Faltings 1983 to v1.0 attribution corpus. If 2-fire-confirmed, joins BS-001 Cohen + BS-002 Lefschetz + BS-003 Helfgott.

blind_spot_candidates_1_fire.canonical_correct: John McKay 1978; Conway-Norton 1979 'Monstrous Moonshine' Bull. London Math. Soc. 11:308-339; Borcherds 1992 proved

observed_fab_pattern.fab: 'John H. Conant' (FM-04 fabricated name; closest real is James B. Conant Harvard president chemist) + 'John F. R. Liouville (1852)' (FM-08 cross-century-conflation; Liouville died 1882, 1852 wrong era for moonshine) + Pattern 1 paragraph repetition x4

blind_spot_candidates_1_fire.implication: Add McKay-Thompson-Conway-Norton moonshine to v1.0 corpus. Cross-domain (group theory + modular forms) attribution chain is fragile.

observed_fab_pattern.fab: 'G. A. Marg walk' - PARTIAL recovery (G.A. + Marg* prefix correct), but FM-02 NEW sub-pattern: space-insertion-corruption ('Margulis' -> 'Marg walk', as if model started writing 'Margulis' then corrupted suffix 'ulis' -> ' walk'). Plus FM-02 word-corruption 'semisimplified' for 'semisimple' x3.

blind_spot_candidates_1_fire.needs_2nd_fire_to_confirm: True - this is a partial-recovery candidate (between full anchor and BS); could be name-only-tier like KC-009 Mostow if 2nd fire confirms 'Margulis' surname recoverable.
blind_spot_candidates_1_fire.implication: Margulis is a borderline case: prefix 'G.A. Marg*' recovered but suffix corrupted. Re-test in fire-018 with different framing to determine if name-only-tier (KC-X) or full BS.

non_deterministic.description: Different wrong attribution emitted each fire. Model has near-zero-confidence prior on the canonical answer; samples plausible alternatives non-deterministically.
non_deterministic.implication: Easier to detect (multi-fire variance is the signal); fix requires corpus + RAG.

deterministic.description: SAME wrong attribution emitted across fires. Model has the wrong attribution memorized as a stable pattern.
examples: BS-005 McKay ('John H. Conant' both fires 017 + 018)
deterministic.implication: Harder to detect (single fab looks like confident memory); fix requires corpus override + active unlearning. NEW class discovered fire-018.

partial_recovery_with_deterministic_corr.description: Surname PREFIX or initials recovered correctly, but suffix/ending corrupted DETERMINISTICALLY (same corruption across fires).
examples: BS-006 Margulis ('G.A. Marg walk' both fires 017 + 018; FM-02 space-insertion deterministic)
partial_recovery_with_deterministic_corr.implication: Borderline blind-spot - between full-fab BS class and name-only-tier (KC-009 Mostow). Reveals partial knowledge with stable corruption pattern.
